title: gouldia terminalis var. ovata
intro: Gouldia terminalis var. ovata, also known as the Hawaiian oak or olapa, is a rare and unique plant species native to the island of Kauai in Hawaii. It is a small tree or shrub with a broad, rounded crown and has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Gouldia terminalis var. ovata include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting immune function, making it a valuable herb for overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Gouldia terminalis var. ovata can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
